data.title: The probability of "Israel launching an attack on Yemen before March 31, 2026" has increased significantly on Polymarket.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of Israel launching an attack on Yemen before March 31, 2026, has increased significantly on Polymarket. The current probability is 60%, compared to an average of 18.1% over the past 7 days. On March 28, 2026, the Houthi rebels announced a ballistic missile attack on southern Israel. This is the first direct military action by the Houthis against Israeli territory since the recent conflict between Iran and the US and Israel. The group claimed to have struck "sensitive military facilities" in southern Israel. The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) confirmed detecting a missile from Yemen and stated that it successfully intercepted it, with no casualties reported so far. Houthi spokesman Yahya Sarreya stated that this action was in response to US and Israeli military operations against targets in Iran, Lebanon, Iraq, and Palestine, emphasizing that their actions will continue until the "aggression stops." Houthi officials have recently issued multiple warnings, stating that blocking the Bab el-Mandeb Strait is one of their "feasible options." Analysts worry that if the Bab el-Mandeb Strait is also effectively blocked, following restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz, it will sever key oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) shipping routes from the Middle East to Europe and Asia. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the forecasting market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Increased Likelihood of Israeli Strike on Yemen by 2026
data.description:
The probability of Israel launching a strike on Yemen by March 31, 2026, has significantly increased on Polymarket, according to Odaily. The current probability stands at 60%, compared to an average of 18.1% over the past week.

On March 28, 2026, Houthi forces announced a ballistic missile attack on southern Israel, marking their first direct military action against Israeli territory amid recent conflicts involving Iran, the U.S., and Israel. The group claimed to have targeted 'sensitive military facilities' in southern Israel. The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) confirmed detecting a missile launched from Yemen and reported successfully intercepting it, with no casualties reported.

Houthi spokesperson Yahya Saree stated that the attack was a response to U.S. and Israeli military actions against targets in Iran, Lebanon, Iraq, and Palestine, emphasizing that their operations would continue until 'aggression stops.'

Houthi officials have repeatedly warned that blocking the Mandeb Strait is a viable option. Analysts express concern that if the Mandeb Strait is effectively blocked following restrictions on the Hormuz Strait, it could disrupt key oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) routes from the Middle East to Europe and Asia.

data.title: Two more liquefied petroleum gas tankers bound for India passed through the Strait of Hormuz and left the Persian Gulf.
data.description: Two more oil tankers carrying liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) bound for India have passed through the Strait of Hormuz and left the Persian Gulf. According to ship tracking data, the Indian-flagged tankers BW Tyr and BW Elm, fully loaded with LPG, were last seen passing the northern tip of the Omani peninsula. Throughout the voyage, both ships used transponders to signal that they were Indian-owned and had Indian crews, a standard safety measure. India is heavily reliant on LPG imports from the Persian Gulf and is facing a severe shortage of this fuel. (Jinshi)

data.title: A wallet transferred out over $200,000 worth of PTB tokens, suggesting a possible sell-off at a high point.
data.description: According to onchainschool monitoring, wallet "0xD4d8" transferred 80.293 million PTB tokens to another wallet "0xF4bFB" 20 minutes ago, worth approximately $219,000. Subsequently, wallet "0xF4bFB" transferred 74.819 million PTB to a CEX, seemingly preparing to sell at a high point. The spot and futures prices of PTB had previously shown unusual fluctuations. Market data shows that the current spot price is $0.0028333, a 24-hour increase of 78.6%.

data.title: Atlas Scout Program Targets Student Investors in Web3, AI, and Biotech
data.description: YZi Labs posted on X that it is collaborating with BNB Chain to launch the Atlas Scout Program aimed at student investors interested in Web3, AI, and biotech sectors. The initiative, led by Ella Zhang, will visit several prestigious universities. The program will be held at Columbia University on March 28, Harvard on March 30, MIT on March 31, UC Berkeley on April 7, and Stanford on April 8. The Atlas Scout Program seeks to engage students in emerging technologies and investment opportunities.

data.title: University Students Encouraged to Apply for Opportunities
data.description: Students from prestigious universities such as Stanford, Harvard, MIT, Columbia, NYU, Carnegie Mellon, and UC Berkeley are being encouraged to apply for new opportunities. YZi Labs posted on X. The initiative aims to engage students in various programs and projects, providing them with valuable experiences and potential career advancements. Interested individuals are invited to submit their applications to participate in these offerings.

data.title: PTB Spot Price Shows Significant Premium Over Contract Price
data.description: The latest market data indicates that the spot price of PTB is currently $0.0028, while the contract price stands at $0.001764. According to Odaily, this reflects a premium of approximately 50% for the spot price over the contract price.

data.title: Latin American Governments Face Challenges Amid Global Oil-Price Surge
data.description: Latin American governments, ranging from Panama to Chile, are experiencing difficulties due to the global oil-price surge. Bloomberg posted on X that these nations, which had aligned politically with U.S. President Donald Trump, are now facing economic repercussions stemming from the conflict between the United States and Iran. The increase in oil prices is impacting their economies, creating challenges for these governments as they navigate the effects of the geopolitical tensions.

data.title: Goliath Ventures Files for Bankruptcy Amid Ponzi Scheme Allegations
data.description:
Goliath Ventures, a cryptocurrency company based in Florida, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in the Southern District of Florida. According to Foresight News, the company is allegedly involved in a Ponzi scheme valued at $328 million, affecting over 2,000 investors. Among the victims, Gregory Wilson reportedly lost approximately $8.74 million, while John Euliano suffered losses of around $1.28 million.

Earlier this month, a class-action lawsuit was filed against JPMorgan Chase, accusing the bank of ignoring suspicious transactions related to Goliath Ventures.

Previously, Foresight News reported that former CEO of Goliath Ventures, Christopher Alexander Delgado, was arrested on charges of wire fraud and money laundering in connection with the alleged Ponzi scheme. Delgado is accused of misleading investors by promising monthly returns through investments in cryptocurrency liquidity pools, while funds were allegedly used to pay returns to earlier investors, purchase luxury homes, and finance lavish activities.

data.title: PTB spot prices are at a premium of approximately 50% over contract prices.
data.description: Market data shows that the spot price of PTB is currently at $0.0028, while the contract price is at $0.001764, with the spot price at a premium of approximately 50% over the contract price.

data.title: Goldman Sachs analysts say Bitcoin prices may have bottomed out of this cycle, but trading volume may decline further.
data.description: In a research report, Goldman Sachs analyst James Yaro stated that the decline in Bitcoin and the crypto market has roughly reached the historical average level between the peak and trough of this cycle. In recent weeks, Bitcoin and crypto-related stocks have shown volatile but relatively stable performance. However, Yaro warned that trading volume may decline further, and in a low-volume environment, Bitcoin prices are prone to sharp fluctuations, making any rebound unsustainable. He pointed out that trading volume typically bottoms out and only shows a significant recovery after about three months. If trading volume declines further, crypto company revenue could decrease by 2% and profits by 4% in 2026. Goldman Sachs currently maintains a "buy" rating on Robinhood, Figure Technologies, and Coinbase, all three companies whose stock prices have fallen at least 50% from their all-time highs. Yaro stated that digital asset-related assets are presenting increasingly attractive entry points. Last month, Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon revealed at the World Liberty Forum hosted by Trump in Mar-a-Lago, Florida, that he holds a small amount of Bitcoin, a shift from his 2024 stance. This week, Bitcoin prices fell back to around $60,000. David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation, pointed out that Bitcoin had previously encountered resistance and fallen back around $72,000. Currently, the daily MACD indicator is flattening at a neutral level, and the short-term trend remains unclear.

data.title: Bitcoin's Potential Short Squeeze: $1.8 Billion in Short Positions at Risk
data.description: Bitcoin is currently facing a potential short squeeze as its price approaches $69,385, putting approximately $1.8 billion in short positions at risk of liquidation. According to NS3.AI, if Bitcoin's price drops below $62,968, around $1.57 billion in long positions could be liquidated instead. This situation highlights the volatility and uncertainty in the cryptocurrency market, as traders closely monitor price movements to manage their positions effectively.

data.title: Israel Intercepts Iranian Missile Attack Amid Rising Tensions
data.description: On March 28, the Israeli Defense Forces detected a new wave of missiles launched by Iran towards Israel. According to BlockBeats, air raid sirens sounded across central and southern regions of Israel. Additionally, Israeli military forces intercepted the missiles over Jerusalem.

data.title: XRP Price Unaffected by Institutional Activity Despite $1.4 Billion ETF Inflows
data.description: XRP's price appears to be disconnected from institutional activity, according to recent analysis. According to NS3.AI, more than $1.4 billion has flowed into XRP ETFs since November 2025. Despite this significant inflow, the price of XRP has not shown a corresponding increase. Notably, Goldman Sachs holds $153.8 million across four spot XRP ETF positions, highlighting substantial institutional interest. However, this interest has yet to translate into a noticeable impact on XRP's market value.

data.title: Bhutan Liquidates $120 Million in Bitcoin, Reducing Reserves by 60%
data.description: Bhutan has significantly reduced its Bitcoin holdings, liquidating approximately $120 million since the beginning of the year. According to NS3.AI, this move has decreased Bhutan's digital reserves by over 60% from their peak levels. Recently, 123.7 BTC, valued at around $8.5 million, was transferred to a previously unused address following a larger divestment of 519.7 BTC just 48 hours earlier. Bhutan's Bitcoin holdings have dropped from a high of 13,000 BTC to approximately 4,300 BTC.

data.title: Tokenization Gains Traction Among Wall Street Institutions
data.description: Wall Street banks, exchanges, regulators, and lawmakers are increasingly focusing on tokenization as a key market-structure initiative. According to NS3.AI, BMO is planning to introduce tokenized cash to facilitate 24/7 margin activities, while Nasdaq has received clearance to support certain tokenized stocks and ETFs. Recent regulatory and congressional actions in the U.S. indicate that tokenization is progressing beyond pilot programs, moving towards establishing rules for real-time settlement and collateral utilization.

data.title: Google Sets 2029 Deadline for Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
data.description: Google has announced a 2029 deadline to transition its authentication services to post-quantum cryptography. According to NS3.AI, this move has intensified scrutiny on blockchains that continue to depend on elliptic-curve signatures. Android 17, Chrome, and Google Cloud have already implemented or support post-quantum protections. Ethereum has outlined an eight-year roadmap featuring weekly developer networks and four hard-fork milestones. In contrast, Bitcoin has yet to establish a coordinated migration plan.

data.title: Market Analysis Suggests Potential Short-Term Bottom for U.S. Stocks
data.description: Citrini Research, the author of the 'End of Day Report,' has shared insights on the market outlook. According to BlockBeats, the analysis suggests that U.S. stocks might reach a short-term bottom on Monday. Furthermore, Citrini Research indicates that the S&P 500 index is unlikely to achieve long-term stability until it falls below 6000 points, although some significant short-term rebounds may occur.

data.title: Citron: US stocks may see a short-term bottom on Monday.
data.description: Citron Research stated in an article on the X platform that the market will not fall in a straight line. The S&P 500 will not stabilize for a long time before falling below 6000 points, but there will be some sharp short-term rebounds in between. US stocks may see a short-term bottom on Monday.
